mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding economy, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth is positive, but SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS 3-month annualized growth exceeds 5%, but SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is low and the federal funds rate is also low, it signals an accommodative monetary environment that supports equity valuations by reducing the cost of capital and increasing the present value of future earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
regime_hint: low inflation, low interest rate environment
failure_condition: If the specified low inflation and low FEDFUNDS conditions are met, but SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 35% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate during an economic contraction signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which will negatively impact consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months during a declared recession, but SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts indicate robust construction activity and consumer confidence in large purchases, which stimulates related industries and overall economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST 3-month average exceeds 800k, but SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, signaling strong underlying economic activity and corporate profitability, which drives S&P 500 forward returns higher.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3%, but SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with high inflation indicates a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing overheating, which typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate earnings expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 3.0% and CPIAUCSL 12-month % change > 4.0%, but SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 30% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth indicates a robust and expanding economy, which underpins corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth exceeds 2.5%, but SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 35% of triggered periods over a 5-year rolling window.
